name: Limpa
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Orange peel, fresh or candied, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast and sugar in the water in a large bowl and let proof for five minutes.
2. Combine the beer, honey (adjust sweetness to preference), butter, and salt in another bowl, stirring well.
3. Add this mixture to the yeast and water mixture.
4. Stir in the spices (cardamom, caraway seeds, aniseed) and orange peel.
5. Gradually add the rye and unbleached flour mixtures into the liquid, stirring continuously until a stiff but still sticky dough forms.
6. Cover with a tea towel and let rise in a warm, dark place for about one hour.
7. Turn out onto a very well-floured surface and work the dough until smooth and elastic. Add more flour to the surface as needed.
8. Shape into a ball, oil the surface, and place in an oiled bowl. Cover with the tea towel again and let rise a second time for about one hour.
9. Punch down the dough, shape into two balls, and place on a greased baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal.
10. Brush with melted butter, cover loosely with waxed paper, and refrigerate for three hours.
11. Remove from the fridge and let sit at room temperature, uncovered, for ten to fifteen minutes before baking in a 375°F oven until hollow when tapped (about 40-45 minutes).
12. Cool before slicing.
adapted from Beard on Bread
